verse.arabic_text: …إِذْ يَقُولُ لِصَٰحِبِهِۦ لَا تَحْزَنْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَنَا ۖ…
verse.translation: ...when he said to his companion, 'Do not grieve; indeed, Allah is with us.'...

theme_question.correct: Finding solace and strength in the knowledge of Allah's constant presence, even in moments of fear.
options: The importance of having many companions for safety.
Finding solace and strength in the knowledge of Allah's constant presence, even in moments of fear.
The prohibition of feeling any sadness or grief as a believer.
The obligation to physically fight in all circumstances.

educational_notes.verse_context: This iconic phrase comes from a verse describing the Hijrah (migration) of Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and his companion Abu Bakr As-Siddiq (RA) from Makkah to Madinah. While hiding from their persecutors in the Cave of Thawr, Abu Bakr became anxious that they would be discovered. The Prophet (ﷺ) comforted him with these words, expressing complete and unwavering trust in Allah's protection.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is Tawakkul (absolute reliance on Allah) and Ma'iyyah (the concept of Allah's divine presence and support). It teaches that true security is not found in physical protection or numbers, but in the unwavering conviction that Allah is with the believers. This verse is a powerful remedy for fear and anxiety, reminding us to find strength and tranquility in our connection with Allah, especially during times of immense hardship.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah At-Tawbah (The Repentance) is the only surah that does not begin with the Bismillah. Revealed in the 9th year of Hijrah, it is a Medinan surah that deals with the final phase of the establishment of the Islamic state. It addresses political and military matters, including the dissolution of treaties with polytheists who repeatedly broke them, the campaign of Tabuk, and a stern warning to the hypocrites. The surah also emphasizes the importance of sincere repentance, charity (Zakat), and striving in the path of Allah.
